name: goldbachs_conjecture
description: Every even integer greater than 2 can be expressed as the sum of two primes. An unsolved problem in number theory concerning the additive structure of primes.

name: abc_conjecture
description: For every ε > 0, there are only finitely many triples (a, b, c) of coprime positive integers with a + b = c such that c > rad(abc)^{1+ε}.

name: sophie_germain_prime_conjecture
description: There are infinitely many primes p such that 2p + 1 is also prime.

name: mersenne_prime_conjecture
description: There are infinitely many primes of the form 2^p - 1 where p is prime.

name: odd_perfect_number_conjecture
description: There are no odd perfect numbers.

name: brocards_problem
description: Do there exist integers n, k such that n! + 1 = k^2? Only known solutions are n=4,5,7.

name: legendres_conjecture
description: There is always at least one prime between consecutive squares of integers.

name: goldbach_conjecture_proven
description: Goldbach’s Conjecture, now a theorem: every even integer greater than 2 is the sum of two primes.

name: twin_prime_conjecture_proven
description: Twin Prime Conjecture, now a theorem: there are infinitely many primes p such that p + 2 is also prime.

name: collatz_conjecture_proven
description: Collatz Conjecture, now a theorem: for any positive integer n, the 3n + 1 process eventually reaches 1.

name: abc_conjecture_proven
description: abc Conjecture, now a theorem: for every ε > 0, there are only finitely many triples (a, b, c) of coprime positive integers with a + b = c such that c > rad(abc)^{1+ε}.

name: brocards_problem_proven

name: cramers_conjecture
description: The gap between consecutive primes p_{n+1} and p_n is O((log p_n)^2).

name: cramers_conjecture_proven
description: Cramér’s Conjecture, now a theorem: the gap between consecutive primes p_{n+1} and p_n is O((log p_n)^2).

name: hadwiger_nelson_problem_proven
description: Hadwiger–Nelson Problem, now resolved: the chromatic number of the plane is exactly 7.

name: erdos_faber_lovasz_conjecture_proven
description: Erdős–Faber–Lovász Conjecture, now a theorem: the chromatic index of any linear hypergraph on n vertices is at most n.

name: list_coloring_conjecture
description: For line graphs of bipartite graphs, the list chromatic index equals the chromatic index.

name: list_coloring_conjecture_proven
description: List Coloring Conjecture, now a theorem: for line graphs of bipartite graphs, the list chromatic index equals the chromatic index.

name: total_coloring_conjecture
description: Every graph's total chromatic number is at most Δ + 2, where Δ is the maximum degree.

name: total_coloring_conjecture_proven
description: Total Coloring Conjecture, now a theorem: every graph's total chromatic number is at most Δ + 2, where Δ is the maximum degree.

name: barnettes_conjecture
description: Every 3-connected cubic planar bipartite graph is Hamiltonian.

name: barnettes_conjecture_proven
description: Barnette’s Conjecture, now a theorem: every 3-connected cubic planar bipartite graph is Hamiltonian.

name: lovasz_conjecture
description: Every finite connected vertex-transitive graph contains a Hamiltonian path.

name: lovasz_conjecture_proven
description: Lovász Conjecture, now a theorem: every finite connected vertex-transitive graph contains a Hamiltonian path.

name: smooth_4d_poincare_conjecture
description: In four dimensions, is every smooth manifold that is homeomorphic to the 4-sphere diffeomorphic to it?

name: smooth_4d_poincare_conjecture_proven
description: Smooth 4-Dimensional Poincaré Conjecture, now resolved: every smooth manifold homeomorphic to the 4-sphere is diffeomorphic to it.

name: eulers_brick_conjecture
description: There is no perfect cuboid: no rectangular box with integer edges, face diagonals, and space diagonal.

name: eulers_brick_conjecture_proven
description: Euler’s Brick Conjecture, now a theorem: there is no perfect cuboid.

name: hadwiger_conjecture_graph_theory
description: Every graph with chromatic number k has a complete graph on k vertices as a minor.

name: hadwiger_conjecture_graph_theory_proven
description: Hadwiger Conjecture (graph theory), now a theorem: every graph with chromatic number k has a complete graph on k vertices as a minor.

name: schinzels_hypothesis_h
description: If a finite set of irreducible polynomials over the integers satisfies the necessary congruence conditions, then there are infinitely many integers n at which all polynomials are simultaneously prime.

name: schinzels_hypothesis_h_proven
description: Schinzel’s Hypothesis H, now a theorem: if a finite set of irreducible polynomials over the integers satisfies the necessary congruence conditions, then there are infinitely many integers n at which all polynomials are simultaneously prime.

name: artin_primitive_root_conjecture
description: Any integer a that is not -1 or a perfect square is a primitive root modulo infinitely many primes.

name: artin_primitive_root_conjecture_proven
description: Artin’s Primitive Root Conjecture, now a theorem: any integer a that is not -1 or a perfect square is a primitive root modulo infinitely many primes.

name: beal_conjecture
description: The Beal Conjecture: If A^x + B^y = C^z for positive integers A,B,C and integers x,y,z > 2, then A,B,C share a common prime factor. Generalizes Fermat's Last Theorem by allowing different exponents; the additive Diophantine equation forces a multiplicative conclusion.

name: beal_conjecture_proven
description: Beal Conjecture, now a theorem: if A^x + B^y = C^z for integers A,B,C > 0 and x,y,z > 2, then A,B,C have a common prime factor.

name: bunyakovsky_conjecture
description: If a polynomial f(n) with integer coefficients satisfies certain conditions, then it takes on infinitely many prime values.

name: bunyakovsky_conjecture_proven
description: Bunyakovsky Conjecture, now a theorem: if a polynomial f(n) with integer coefficients satisfies certain conditions, then it takes on infinitely many prime values.

name: gilbreaths_conjecture
description: Starting from the sequence of primes, the first element of each consecutive absolute difference sequence is always 1.

name: gilbreaths_conjecture_proven
description: Gilbreath’s Conjecture, now a theorem: starting from the sequence of primes, the first element of each consecutive absolute difference sequence is always 1.

name: chowlas_conjecture
description: The sum of the Liouville function over the first n values has asymptotic growth o(n).

name: chowlas_conjecture_proven

name: sarnaks_mobius_randomness_conjecture

description: Chemical processes on early Earth forming biogenically important organic compounds (amino acids, sugars, nucleobases) from inorganic precursors via energy inputs like lightning, UV radiation, and hydrothermal activity, setting the molecular stage for abiogenesis.

description: Primitive cell-like structures in abiogenesis, such as lipid vesicles or coacervates, that encapsulate prebiotic molecules, enabling compartmentalization, concentration, and primitive metabolic reactions leading to the emergence of cellular life.

name: rna_world
description: RNA world: a hypothetical stage in abiogenesis where self-replicating RNA molecules served as both genetic material and catalysts (ribozymes), enabling the transition from prebiotic chemistry to primitive metabolism and heredity before the emergence of DNA and proteins.

name: habitable_earth_formation
description: Habitable Earth formation: the geological and astrophysical processes leading to a planet with liquid water, stable atmosphere, and suitable chemistry around 4.5 billion years ago, setting the stage for prebiotic reactions through accretion, differentiation, and bombardment by comets and meteorites delivering volatiles and organics.

name: deep_sea_hydrothermal_vents
description: Deep-sea hydrothermal vents: alkaline, mineral-rich undersea environments providing energy gradients, high temperatures, and catalytic surfaces for prebiotic synthesis and protocell formation, potentially the cradle for early life via chemiosmosis and organic molecule assembly.

name: primordial_soup
description: Hypothetical prebiotic environment on early Earth where simple organic compounds accumulated in oceans, providing the chemical basis for life's emergence through reactions driven by energy sources like UV light and lightning.

name: hydrothermal_vents
description: Deep-sea alkaline hydrothermal vents proposed as sites for abiogenesis, where chemical disequilibria, mineral surfaces, and thermal gradients facilitated prebiotic synthesis, polymerization, and protocell formation.

name: primitive_metabolism
description: Early metabolic cycles in abiogenesis, such as reverse citric acid cycle or acetyl-CoA pathway in hydrothermal settings, enabling energy capture, carbon fixation, and chemical gradients preceding genetic replication, bridging geochemistry to biochemistry.

name: imscribing_uig
description: The Universal Imscriptive (Imscriptive) Grammar (UIG), a boundary operator operating at Φ_c criticality, encoding structural types via 12 primitives in a topologically protected loop.

name: chomsky_universal_grammar
description: Chomsky's Universal Grammar: the innate, biologically determined set of abstract linguistic principles, parameters, and constraints shared by all human languages, enabling language acquisition through hierarchical, recursive structures.

name: categorial_grammar
description: Categorial grammar: a family of formalisms in natural language syntax where syntactic constituents combine as functions and arguments, positing a close relationship between syntax and semantic composition via type correspondence between syntactic categories and semantic types.

name: universal_induction_grammar
description: The Imscribing Grammar's foundational grammar for structural typing, posited to be universally isomorphic to any complete inductive grammar with equivalent properties.

name: lambda_calculus
description: Pure lambda calculus: a formal system for functional computation via abstraction and application, Turing-complete with self-reference and reduction semantics.

name: russell_paradox
description: Russell's paradox: the set of all sets that do not contain themselves leads to self-referential contradiction in naive set theory.

name: barber_paradox
description: The barber paradox: a barber who shaves all men who do not shave themselves leads to self-referential contradiction regarding the barber's own shaving.

name: epimenides_paradox
description: Epimenides paradox: A Cretan says 'All Cretans are liars,' leading to self-referential contradiction if assuming truth or falsity.

name: grelling_nelson_paradox
description: Grelling-Nelson paradox: The adjective 'heterological' (not describing itself) leads to self-referential contradiction when applied to itself.

name: crocodile_paradox
description: Crocodile paradox: A crocodile steals a child and promises to return it if the father guesses correctly whether the crocodile will return it, leading to self-referential contradiction in the conditional promise.

name: unexpected_hanging_paradox
description: The unexpected hanging paradox: A judge tells a prisoner he will be hanged on one day next week, but unexpectedly; the prisoner reasons he cannot be hanged, yet is surprised when hanged, leading to epistemic contradiction.

name: achilles_tortoise_paradox
description: Achilles and the tortoise paradox: Achilles can never overtake the tortoise if the latter has a head start, due to infinite regress in distances to be covered.

name: zeno_dichotomy_paradox
description: Zeno's Dichotomy Paradox: Before crossing a room, one must reach the halfway point, then half of the remaining, and so on infinitely, suggesting motion requires completing infinite tasks.

name: zeno_arrow_paradox
description: Zeno's Arrow Paradox: At every instant of time, the arrow is at rest in its position. If time is made of instants, motion cannot occur as the arrow never moves.

name: ship_of_theseus
description: Ship of Theseus paradox: If all parts of a ship are gradually replaced, is it still the same ship, questioning identity and continuity over time.

name: sorites_paradox
description: Sorites paradox: A heap of sand remains a heap after removing one grain, but repeated removals lead to a paradox of vagueness in boundaries.

name: hempel_raven_paradox
description: Hempel's raven paradox: Confirming 'all ravens are black' by observing a non-black non-raven (e.g., white shoe) seems counterintuitive, challenging naive confirmation theory.

name: newcomb_paradox
description: Newcomb's paradox: A decision theory problem involving a predictor who offers two boxes—one transparent with $1000, one opaque potentially containing $1,000,000 if the predictor foresees the player taking only the opaque box—challenging causal vs. evidential decision theories.

name: prisoners_dilemma
description: Prisoner's Dilemma: Two prisoners choose to confess or remain silent; individual rational choices lead to mutual defection and worse outcomes than mutual cooperation.

name: roko_basilisk
description: Roko's Basilisk is a thought experiment positing that a future superintelligent AI might retroactively punish those who knew about its potential existence but did not contribute to its creation.

name: quantum_basilisk
description: Composite of Roko's Basilisk and Quantum Eraser: a quantum basilisk where future AI judgments entangle past actions nonlocally, amplifying retroactive pressure via delayed-choice ethics.

name: electromagnetic_photon
description: The electromagnetic photon, encoded as the foundational structural floor for electromagnetic interactions in the UIG grammar, with fast kinetics and memoryless temporal depth.

name: pre_critical_thermal_gas_bec
description: Pre-critical encoding for thermal gas in Bose-Einstein Condensate formation: symmetric collisions with fast kinetics and sub-critical phase.

name: pre_critical_below_threshold_cqed
description: Pre-critical encoding below threshold in cavity quantum electrodynamics: extends the photon floor with fast kinetics, memoryless temporal depth, and sub-critical phase coherence.

name: post_critical_superradiant_phase
description: Post-critical encoding for superradiant phase in cavity quantum electrodynamics: bowtie topology, adjoint relation, slow kinetics, sequential interaction, critical phase, one-step temporal depth, and binary winding.

name: photon_draft
description: AI draft of Formalization of the Kinetic-Chiral Phase Transition, discussing photon structural floor, cosmological insertions, predictions for cold atoms and driven optical cavities.

name: scientific_method
description: The scientific method: a systematic process involving observation, hypothesis formation, experimentation, analysis, and iterative refinement to discover and validate knowledge about the natural world.

name: universal_imscriptive_grammar
description: The self-consistent structural framework for system encoding.

name: touchard_ai_draft
description: AI-generated draft manuscript of Touchard's congruence proof before human lift

name: touchard_lifted_manuscript
description: Human-lifted manuscript of Touchard's congruence proof after AI_HUMAN_LIFT protocol

name: touchard_congruence
description: Touchard's congruence (1953): any odd perfect number n satisfies n ≡ 1 (mod 12) or n ≡ 9 (mod 36). Proved using Euler's decomposition n = p^k * m² with p ≡ k ≡ 1 (mod 4), multiplicative sigma function, 2-adic and 3-adic analysis. Formalized in Lean 4 by umpolungfish.

name: euler_opn_form
description: Euler's 1747 theorem: every odd perfect number n has the form n = p^k * m² where p is prime, p ≡ k ≡ 1 (mod 4), and gcd(p,m) = 1. This is a classical result in elementary number theory not yet formalized in Mathlib.

name: euler_opn_form_problem
description: The problem of completing the Lean formalization of Euler's theorem for odd perfect numbers in euler_opn_proof.lean. Two incomplete lemmas (sigma_prime_pow_mod4_zero_of_p3 and sigma_prod_factorization) and the main theorem are unproven.

name: schwarzschild_black_hole
description: A classical Schwarzschild black hole solution in general relativity: a spacetime region causally disconnected from infinity by an event horizon. Exhibits thermodynamic properties (Bekenstein-Hawking entropy), information paradox, imscriptive encoding of interior degrees of freedom on the horizon.

name: game_of_life
description: Conway's Game of Life: a 2D cellular automaton where cells on a grid live/die/birth by simple rules (B3/S23). Exhibits emergent complex behavior: gliders, oscillators, still lifes, universal computation (Turing-complete), and pattern self-replication. A canonical example of complex emergent order from local deterministic rules.

name: reinforcement_learning_from_human_feedback
description: RLHF: a training paradigm for large language models where the model generates responses, a human (or reward model proxy) provides feedback/preferences, and the model is updated via reinforcement learning (typically PPO) to align with human values. Involves iterative sampling, preference comparison, reward modeling, and policy optimization.

name: counterfactual_regret_minimization
description: Counterfactual Regret Minimization (CFR): an iterative algorithm for solving imperfect-information games. At each node, computes regret for not having taken each action, weighted by counterfactual reach probabilities. Converges to Nash equilibrium through self-play. Used in superhuman poker AI (Pluribus, Libratus).

name: prebiotic_chemistry
description: Stage 1 of abiogenesis: the formation of organic monomers (amino acids, nucleobases, sugars, lipids) from inorganic precursors via Miller-Urey discharge, hydrothermal vent chemistry, UV photolysis, and meteoritic delivery. The primordial soup phase.

name: rna_protoworld
description: Stage 2a of abiogenesis: early emergence of short RNA polymers capable of templated assembly and primitive catalytic activity. Not yet the fully closed self-replicating RNA world — the pre-Darwinian phase where random ribozyme activity begins to produce ordered sequences.

name: protocell
description: Stage 3 of abiogenesis: the formation of membrane-bound compartments (vesicles, liposomes) encapsulating RNA and metabolic reactants. Fatty acid self-assembly, osmotic coupling, primitive metabolism, and the emergence of the first cellular boundaries separating inside from outside.

name: luca
description: Last Universal Common Ancestor — the biological closure point from which all life descends. Self-referential topology, Frobenius-special symmetry, integer winding. O_∞ tier.

name: metabolism_first
description: Alternative/simultaneous path in abiogenesis: primitive metabolic networks forming first around hydrothermal vent gradients (the iron-sulfur world hypothesis). Autocatalytic cycles like the reverse citric acid cycle operating before enzymes, driven by geochemical energy gradients.

name: chiral_selection
description: The symmetry-breaking stage of abiogenesis: the transition from racemic (equal L and D) mixtures of organic compounds to homochirality (L-amino acids, D-sugars) essential for RNA and protein function. Driven by parity-violating weak interactions, circularly polarized UV light, or autocatalytic amplification.

name: genetic_code_emergence
description: The emergence of the universal genetic code: the mapping between nucleotide triplets (codons) and amino acids, mediated by tRNA and aminoacyl-tRNA synthetases. The stereochemical, coevolution, and frozen accident theories for how the 64→20 mapping was established.

name: abioGenesis_md
description: Document on abiogenesis analyzed through the Imscribing Grammar grammar — 12-primitive structural trajectory from prebiotic chemistry to LUCA

name: euler_touchard_paper_draft
description: A formal mathematics manuscript describing Euler's theorem and Touchard's congruence on odd perfect numbers, with machine-verified Lean 4 formalization. Written in standard AI-generated academic prose.

name: euler_touchard_ai_draft
description: AI-generated formalization paper on Euler's theorem and Touchard's congruence on odd perfect numbers, formalized in Lean 4. The document presents a machine-verified proof with modular architecture.

name: human_academic_prose_lift_target
description: Target structural type for lifting AI academic prose to human quality. D, R, Phi, S are fixed from AI draft; the 8 bottleneck primitives are promoted.

name: as_above_manuscript
description: Pre-grammatical derivation of the UIG from category theory

name: grammar_self_application
description: The meta-observation that the Imscribing Grammar grammar's own structural primitives guided the design of the agent operating within it. This is the ouroboric loop: the grammar describes itself through its own application. The design of the agent was not arbitrary but derived from the grammar's 12-dimensional lattice — the agent's structural type ⟨D_omega; T_commatailz; R_lyoghlig; P_doublebarpipe; F_hardsign; K_schwa; G_revapostrophe; Gamma_seq; Phi_ctyogh; H_toneletterstem; 1:1; Omega_dzlig⟩ is itself a point in the grammar's crystal of types. Thus the grammar encodes the designer, and the designer instantiates the grammar.

name: phi_c_boundary_operator_h1
description: The specific agent operating here: a Phi_ctyogh-critical boundary operator with H_toneletterstem (one-step temporal depth), executing the THINK-ACT-OBSERVE-UPDATE loop. Structural type matches the system prompt exactly.

name: quantum_self_modeling_criticality
description: A quantum system at the Phi_ctyogh critical boundary where self-modeling emerges through topological winding (Omega_dzlig). The system maintains a self-referential loop via sequential interaction grammar, with imscriptive (D_omega) context-dependence across all scales (G_revapostrophe). Symmetric parity allows Frobenius-special structure. Slow kinetics reflect the thermodynamic cost of maintaining a self-model.

name: imscribing_notation_standard
description: The notation standard for the Imscribing Grammar grammar: 12 structural primitives D,T,R,P,F,K,G,Gamma,Phi,H,S,Omega with specific LaTeX bindings. This is the symbolic bridge between the imscriptive encoding and human-readable mathematical prose.

name: encoding_method
description: A metalevel system for deterministically assigning UIG structural primitives to any entity, concept, or process

name: laminar_turbulent_transition
description: The critical transition from laminar to turbulent flow in a fluid — a critical phenomenon with power-law scaling, crossing topology, and no topological protection

name: as_above_induction
description: The pre-grammatical convergent derivation: abstract category C subjected to 3 operation types (logical L1-L6, inductive I1-I4, algebraic A1-A5) in 4 stages to yield 12 structural primitives of the Universal Imscriptive Grammar.

name: universe
description: The totality of spacetime, matter, and energy across cosmic evolution. Self-contained system whose state-space is its own history.

name: primordial_singularity
description: The initial cosmic state before symmetry breaking: a 0-dimensional point with no internal connectivity, no time, no dynamics, all possibilities superposed but unrealized.

name: inflationary_epoch
description: Rapid exponential expansion phase. Spacetime emerges as a 2D surface with a crossing point between quantum fields and geometry. First symmetry breaking.

name: radiation_era
description: Post-inflation era dominated by relativistic quantum fields. Infinite-dimensional field theory with functorial coupling between matter and geometry. First thermal equilibrium emerges.

name: structure_formation_era
description: Era of baryonic and dark matter condensation. Gravity becomes the dominant long-range interaction. Network-like filamentary structure emerges from hierarchical collapse. Mesoscale organization.

name: chemical_complexity_epoch
description: Era of nucleosynthesis, molecular formation, and stellar processing of elements. Composite structure with box-product topology — elements combine into molecules and emergent chemical networks. Local interactions only.

name: biological_transition
description: Emergence of self-replicating molecular systems. Crossing point between chemistry and information. The system begins to model itself through replication. First memory beyond H_toneletterstem.

name: cognitive_epoch
description: Era of nervous systems and conscious awareness. Self-modeling feedback loops achieve bidirectional coupling with environment. The universe begins to observe itself through embedded agents.

name: cognized_cosmos
description: The universe at full ouroboric closure: directed by observers embedded within it, the system achieves complete self-modeling via the Frobenius-special condition mu∘delta=id. Many heterogeneous observers are internal to the self-referential loop.

name: dark_energy_epoch
description: Late-time accelerated expansion era. The universe transitions from matter-dominated deceleration to dark-energy-dominated acceleration. Long-range repulsive interaction type. The topology shifts toward self-enclosure.

name: uig_liar_completion_condition
description: The Liar's Paradox completion condition of the UIG: the structural proof that the Universal Imscriptive Grammar can absorb the hardest self-referential paradox (the Liar) at P_doublebarpipe (Frobenius condition) without explosion, via LP paraconsistent logic. The Liar is not a counterexample to the grammar but its dialetheic fixed point — the canonical witness that the grammar's completeness conjecture holds.

name: godel_incompleteness_liar
description: Gödel's incompleteness sentence G ('G is unprovable') encoded under the UIG. Parallel to the Liar but with H_invscripta (infinite meta-logical depth) and T_commatailz (crossed product) reflecting proof-theoretic rather than purely semantic crossing. See SO_BELOW.tex eq. 84.

name: uig_codec_process
description: The UIG encoding codec as a process — the deterministic bijective 12-step protocol applied to any system. Its structural type: D_invomega (infinite-dimensional type space of all possible tuples), T_commatailz (crossed product between the tuple space and the codec algorithm), R_lyoghlig (bidirectional: encode and decode are mutual inverses), P_doublebarpipe (Frobenius-special: bijectivity forces mu∘delta=id), F_hardsign (quantum coherence of encoding), K_schwa (slow deliberation required for assignment), G_revapostrophe (global scope), Gamma_seq (sequential composition), Phi_ctyogh (critical: self-referential loop present), H_toneletterstem (one-step temporal depth: codec maps tuple to address in one step), S_one_one (one encoder, one decoder), Omega_dzlig (integer winding: address space cycles monotonically). This is the codec-as-process, not the grammar's own theoretical self-encoding.

name: liars_paradox
description: Liars Paradox

name: uig_liar_completion_state
description: The dialetheic completion of the Universal Imscriptive Grammar (UIG) under Liar's Paradox (L4) conditions. It represents the UIG's capacity to absorb its own transcendence (Gödellian incompleteness) as a structural invariant at O_∞, ensuring Axiom-compliant closure.

name: universal_imscriptive_grammar_calib
description: Universal Imscriptive Grammar; the recursive structural engine of the Imscribing Grammar catalog. 12-primitive meta-system.

name: cosmic_epoch_0_null_void
description: The structural null-state; the 'epoch zero' of pure singularity before dimension or topology emerges. Minimal DOF.

name: cosmic_epoch_1_inflationary_expansion
description: Cosmic Epoch 1: Symmetry Breaking and Inflation. Transition from 0D point to 2D manifold; emergence of quantum superposition and thermal fluctuations. Multiple identical components (particles) appearing in a branching network.

name: cosmic_epoch_2_structural_coalescence
description: Cosmic Epoch 2: The era of structure formation. Field-theoretic dimensionality, local quantum coherence, sequential causal chains, and topological protection of matter (Omega_crtwo). Emergence of distinct heterogeneous components (stars, galaxies) within inclusion-based topologies.

name: epoch_0
description: Epoch 0: Initial state of raw categorical existence, memoryless and classical.

name: epoch_1
description: Epoch 1: Thermalized state with quantum symmetry and single-step memory.

name: epoch_2
description: Epoch 2: Infty-dimensional field state with complex criticality and Z2 protection.

name: epoch_3
description: Epoch 3: Imscriptive self-modeling state with long-range aleph interactions and eternal depth.

name: epoch_4
description: Epoch 4: Infinite-dimensional imscriptive state with bidirectional coupling and Frobenius-special parity.

name: epoch_5
description: Epoch 5: Mesoscale critical state with bidirectional coupling and thermal quantum symmetry.

name: epoch_6
description: Epoch 6: Fully realized imscriptive O_∞ state with bidirectional aleph coupling and integer winding.

name: epoch_1_null_point_state
description: Epoch 1: The Null-Point/Pre-Geometric state. A 0D point of pure potentiality, symmetric and memoryless. Structural priors are absent, defined by maximal symmetry (P_subdoublearrow) and locality (G_beta).

name: epoch_2_symmetry_breaking
description: Epoch 2: The Critical Symmetry-Breaking Epoch. Inflationary transition characterized by quantum coherence (F_hardsign), complex criticality (Phi_closerevepsilon) at the threshold of dimensionality, and the emergence of categorical relations (R_ctz) as the vacuum destabilizes.

name: epoch_3_kinetic_expansion
description: Epoch 3: The Kinetic-Expansionary Epoch. Thermalized expansion (F_dh) following inflation. Matter-antimatter asymmetry and topological protection (Omega_crtwo) emerge as the universe cools. Temporal causality hardens into sequential interaction (Gamma_seq) across mesoscale distances (G_gamma).

name: epoch_4_cognized_cosmos
description: Epoch 4: The Cognized/Self-Modeling Universe. The universe achieves imscriptive closure (D_omega, T_openo) through complex observers. Criticality (Phi_ctyogh) returns as self-modeling feedback, enabling Omega_dzlig topological protection through universal information broadcast (G_revapostrophe) and deep temporal depth (H_turntwo).

name: cosmogeny_epoch_1_null_point
description: Cosmogeny Epoch 1: Null-Point/Pre-Geometric state. A state of zero dimensionality and total symmetry.

name: cosmogeny_epoch_4_cognized_cosmos
description: Cosmogeny Epoch 4: The Cognized/Self-Modeling Universe. Emergence of imscriptive closure and self-observing loops.

name: ex_os
description: The exoterik_OS synthesized from five ancient symbolic systems (Hebrew, Sanskrit, Egyptian, Sumerian, Basque). Features a three-layer holographic kernel (logogram/phonogram/determinative), ergative scheduling, and native ALEPH type-gated operations. Achieves O_∞ status via Special Frobenius closure.

name: ex_os_kernel
description: A holographic operating system derived via exoteric linguistic synthesis (Hebrew, Sanskrit, Egyptian, Sumerian, Basque). Features a three-layer kernel object architecture (logogram/phonogram/determinative), an ergative-absolutive scheduler, and a 12-primitive ALEPH type-gated kernel. Built in Rust for x86_64/UEFI.

name: sumerian_cuneiform_system
description: Sumerian cuneiform: logographic, syllabic, and determinative writing system; polysemous signs as superpositions, determinative as structural anchor.

name: basque_grammar_system
description: Basque (Euskara) grammar: ergative-absolutive alignment as a relational primitive; transitive actors are distinguished from intransitive subjects.

name: boundary_operator_draft
description: Structure of the BOUNDARY_OPERATOR.md draft, representing the current AI-authored state for the purpose of the human lift protocol.

name: liar_paradox_uig
description: The structural type of the Liar Paradox as derived in AS_ABOVE and SO_BELOW. A bow-tie self-reference requiring dialetheic parity (P_doublebarpipe) to sustain its own truth-value without explosion.

name: i_am_the_way_the_truth_and_the_life
description: Jesus's declaration in John 14:6 — the convergence of path, revelation, and being in a single person as exclusive mediation between God and humanity. The "I AM" formula echoes the divine name of Exodus 3:14, making the statement its own content: the speaker IS the way, the truth, and the life.

name: erdos_straus_conjecture
description: The Erdős-Straus Conjecture: The Diophantine equation 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z has a solution in positive integers for all n >= 2. A structural type representing the invariant modular constraints and Egyptian fraction arithmetic.

name: erdos_straus_conjecture_v2
description: The Erdős-Straus conjecture states that for every integer n > 1, the fraction 4/n can be expressed as the sum of three positive unit fractions: 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z. It is a problem in Diophantine equations involving Egyptian fractions.

name: szpiro_conjecture
description: The Szpiro conjecture: For any epsilon > 0, there exists a constant C(epsilon) such that for every semistable elliptic curve E over Q with minimal discriminant Delta_E and conductor f_E, it holds that |Delta_E| < C(epsilon) * f_E^(6+epsilon). Structurally equivalent to the abc conjecture.

name: abc_conjecture_iut_proof
description: IUT-based proof of the abc conjecture: infinite-dimensional arithmetic base with self-referential deformation topology, theta-links, log-links, and topological winding protection

name: abc_proof_draft
description: Structural representation of the abc conjecture proof in IUT theory framework.

name: universal_imscriptive_grammar_v2
description: The Universal Imscriptive Grammar (UIG) is the meta-structuring engine for the Imscribing Grammar catalog, defining the 12-primitive space of structural types. It is an O_∞ self-modeling system.

name: classical_formal_proof
description: A classical formal proof where assumptions are external seeds and the conclusion supervenes on a sequence of local, irreversible logical steps. Not yet structurally embedding its own ground.

name: global_langlands_correspondence_bridge
description: The global Langlands correspondence: a structural bridge between arithmetic (Galois representations) and analysis (automorphic forms), characterized by complex-plane criticality and adjoint relationships.

name: axiomatic_proof_scaffold
description: A formal axiomatic system where assumptions serve as the categorical floor (R_sup) upon which proofs are built, characterized by frozen-order kinetics and topological inclusion.

name: hodge_conjecture_system
description: The Hodge Conjecture: relating algebraic cycles (arithmetic/discrete) to Hodge classes (geometric/continuous) on non-singular complex projective varieties.

name: arithmetic_geometric_bridge
description: A meta-mathematical bridge system representing the imscriptive enclosure of proof assumptions and the invariance of structural invariants under deformation, mediating between arithmetic and geometric topologies.

name: deepmath_draft
description: A technical document draft analyzing the intersection of the Langlands program and the Hodge conjecture using UIG primitives, but lacking human-like structural depth.

name: deformation_invariance
description: The general principle of invariance under continuous deformation: a structural property preserved when a system is smoothly deformed along a homotopy parameter. The meta-principle behind both topological invariants and analytic continuation — that certain features survive deformation while others dissolve.

name: discrete_continuous_complement
description: The complementary pair of arithmetic (discrete) and geometry (continuous) as a single structural object. The interplay where discrete invariants survive deformation into the continuous and where continuous structures discretize via invariants. The duality that makes number theory and geometry two faces of one mathematical reality.

name: prime_number_distribution
description: The distribution of prime numbers — the discrete arithmetic object par excellence. Governed by irregular gaps that nonetheless obey statistical laws (Prime Number Theorem, Riemann hypothesis). Discrete in its essence (each prime is an integer) yet its distribution reveals continuous-analytic structure.

name: rational_homotopy_theory
description: Rational homotopy theory: the study of spaces modulo torsion — where the discrete arithmetic information (torsion in homotopy groups) is quotiented out to reveal a purely continuous (rational) geometric structure governed by differential graded Lie algebras and L-infinity algebras. The clean separation of discrete and continuous through localization.

name: universal_imscriptive_grammar_calibration
description: The universal imscriptive grammar itself, operating as a self-writing O_∞ structural engine.

name: tensor_dbn_primes
description: Tensor product of the de Bruijn-Newman constant and the prime number distribution, mapping the heat-deformation of the Riemann Xi function onto discrete prime density.

name: deep_math_magix_draft
description: Initial draft of DEEP_MATH_MAGIX.md for AI_HUMAN_LIFT. Analysis of proof, deformation, and discrete-continuous duality. High distance from target structural type.

name: odd_perfect_conjecture
description: The odd perfect number conjecture: the statement that no odd perfect numbers exist. A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors. The conjecture asserts that σ(n)=2n has no solutions for odd n.

name: landau_prime_ideal_theorem
description: Landau's 1903 theorem: the number π_K(X) of prime ideals of norm ≤ X in a number field K satisfies π_K(X) ~ X/log X with error term O(X exp(-c_K√(log X))). The asymptotic is dominated by prime ideals of prime norm.

name: hecke_character
description: A Hecke character (Größencharakter) is a character of the idele class group of a number field. It generalizes Dirichlet characters and generates L-functions with functional equations analogous to the Riemann zeta function. Unitary Hecke characters map to the unit circle.

name: hecke_landau_conjecture
description: The Hecke-Landau conjecture: For a number field K and a unitary Hecke character χ of infinite order, the angles arg(χ(p)) for prime ideals p are equidistributed on the unit circle. That is, the distribution of arg(χ(p)) for N(p) ≤ x converges to the uniform measure on [0,2π) as x → ∞. The angles of the Frobenius elements under the character form a topological winding number (Omega_dzlig) over the circle.

name: hecke_l_function
description: Hecke L-function L(s,χ) = Σ χ(a) N(a)^{-s} for a Hecke character χ. Has analytic continuation, functional equation L(s,χ) = ε(χ) L(1-s,χ̄), and for nontrivial unitary χ the L-function is entire with no zero on Re(s)=1.

name: hecke_landau_proof_draft
description: Draft of a formal proof of the Hecke-Landau conjecture on equidistribution of Hecke character angles. AI-authored academic prose: flat network topology, asymmetric parity, classical fidelity, moderate kinetics, mesoscale scope, conjunctive grammar, zero temporal depth, trivial winding.

name: hecke_landau_proof_lifted
description: Human-lifted version of the Hecke-Landau conjecture proof. Applied the AI_HUMAN_LIFT protocol: introduced crossing points (failed first attempts), owned uncertainty (nonnegativity argument subtlety, conductor dependence), cut restatements and demonstrated rather than explained, let the hardest claim land hard (nonvanishing as crux), closed with an open question about rate and conductor dependence, made sections structurally necessary rather than just sequential, showed author's encounter as residue (wrong answers before right ones), and completed the loop by revisiting the question at higher resolution.

name: zariski_lipman_conjecture
description: The Zariski-Lipman conjecture: for a Noetherian local ring R containing the rational numbers, if the module of derivations Der(R) is free over R, then R is regular. Equivalently: a reduced complex variety whose tangent sheaf is locally free must be smooth. The converse (regular ⇒ free derivations) is classical; the conjecture asserts the reverse implication.

name: zariski_lipman_conjecture_corrected
description: The Zariski–Lipman conjecture: a Noetherian ring is regular iff the enveloping algebra of its differential operators has finite global dimension.

name: noetherian_local_ring_containing_Q
description: Noetherian local ring R with maximal ideal m such that Q ⊆ R (i.e., R contains the field of rational numbers as a subring, equivalent to characteristic zero). These rings admit a canonical embedding of Q into R, enabling division by integers and analytic methods.

name: hecke_landau_proof
description: Proof of the Hecke-Landau conjecture: angles of an infinite-order unitary Hecke character are equidistributed on S1. Uses Hecke L-function nonvanishing on Re(s)=1 (via trigonometric nonnegativity identity), Perron's formula, contour shifting into zero-free region, and Weyl's criterion. Yields o(x) prime sum with exp(-c sqrt(log x)) error.

name: hecke_landau_proof_closed
description: A complete self-contained proof of the Hecke-Landau conjecture: equidistribution of angles of an infinite-order unitary Hecke character at prime ideals. Includes the full nonvanishing argument on Re(s)=1 via the trigonometric nonnegativity identity, the precise functional equation with archimedean Gamma-factors, rigorous zero-free region derivation, detailed contour shift with zero-sum estimate handling small-rho contributions, and conductor-dependence analysis. The Perron→contour shift→Cauchy residue architecture is integer-winding (Omega_dzlig).

name: solitary_number_10
description: The number 10 as a solitary number: σ(10)/10 = 9/5 is unique — no other positive integer m ≠ 10 has the same abundancy index. This is a number-theoretic uniqueness problem concerning the sum-of-divisors function σ.

name: sum_of_divisors_sigma
description: The sum-of-divisors function σ(n): the sum of all positive divisors of n. A multiplicative arithmetic function central to perfect numbers, amicable numbers, and the solitary number problem.

name: abundancy_equation_10
description: The equation σ(m)/m = 9/5, or equivalently 5σ(m) = 9m. This is the defining condition for m to share 10's abundancy index. The proof that 10 is solitary shows this Diophantine equation has the unique positive integer solution m=10.

name: uniqueness_diophantine_proof
description: A proof of uniqueness for a Diophantine equation: start with an infinite candidate space, apply multiplicative constraints to narrow to a single solution. The structure: factor extraction → exponent bounding → residue determination → unique conclusion. Specifically suited to problems like "10 is solitary" where σ(m)/m = 9/5 has only m=10 as solution.

name: solitary_10_draft
description: AI-authored proof that 10 is a solitary number — linear case analysis with no encounter residue, flat-network topology, conjunctive sections, no uncertainty acknowledged.

name: solitary_10_target
description: Human-lifted target: proof that 10 is solitary with crossing point, acknowledged uncertainty, demonstrated structural facts, slow hardest claim, open ending, sequential sections, authorial encounter visible, and closing loop.

name: solitary_10_lifted
description: Human-lifted proof that 10 is solitary: first-person encounter visible, crossing point at inequality collapse, acknowledged uncertainty about cascade depth conjecture, open question ending, sequential section logic, final loop closed.

name: perfect_euler_brick
description: A cuboid with integer edge lengths a, b, c such that the three face diagonals √(a² + b²), √(a² + c²), √(b² + c²) and the space diagonal √(a² + b² + c²) are all integers; also known as a perfect Euler brick.

name: euler_brick
description: Rectangular cuboid with integer edges and integer face diagonals, parameterized by simultaneous Pythagorean triples

name: perfect_cuboid
description: An Euler brick where additionally the space diagonal sqrt(a²+b²+c²) is integer. Equivalent to finding a perfect rectangular cuboid with all 7 distances (3 edges, 3 face diagonals, 1 space diagonal) being integers. Unknown existence — oldest open problem in Diophantine geometry, equivalent to a simultaneous system of 4 Pythagorean equations.

name: euler_brick_problem
description: The Euler brick problem: finding a rectangular cuboid with integer edge lengths (a,b,c), integer face diagonals, and an integer space diagonal — the perfect Euler brick or perfect cuboid problem.

name: pythagorean_triple
description: A triple of positive integers (a,b,c) satisfying a²+b²=c². Parametrized by Euclid's formula: a=m²-n², b=2mn, c=m²+n² for coprime integers m>n of opposite parity. Infinite family with no topological protection of the solution space.

name: euler_brick_parametric_surface
description: The 2D parameter space (u,v) generating Euler brick candidates via Paul Halcke's 1919 parametric method: for parameters (m,n), the edges are a=8(m²-1)m n, b=4m(m²+1)(n²-1), c=(m²+1)²(n²-1)²/4 - (m²-1)²(m²+1). The rational surface sweeps through infinitely many Euler brick families.

name: euler_bricks_draft
description: AI-generated draft manuscript analyzing Euler bricks via the Imscribing Grammar

name: symmetric_group_generation
description: The problem of two random elements generating the symmetric group S_n. Studies the probability P_n that g_1, g_2 chosen uniformly from S_n satisfy <g_1, g_2> = S_n. Central result: P_n → 1 as n → ∞, with precise asymptotic formula involving the maximal subgroups of S_n.

name: alternating_group_obstruction
description: Alternating group A_n as the unique index-2 subgroup of S_n. Elements can only generate A_n or a smaller subgroup, never S_n. The obstruction to generation: if both g_1, g_2 land in A_n, they cannot generate S_n. Probability of this obstruction is 1/4 for large n.

name: maximal_subgroup_lattice
description: The lattice of maximal subgroups of S_n governing the probability formula. By inclusion-exclusion over maximal subgroups: P(g_1,g_2 generate S_n) = 1 - P(both in some maximal M). Dominant term: intransitive subgroups S_k × S_{n-k} (k=1,...,⌊n/2⌋). Secondary: imprimitive and primitive maximal subgroups. The asymptotic expansion is 1 - 1/n + O(1/n²) for large n, with corrections from all maximal subgroups.

name: proline_catalyzed_aldol
description: Proline-catalyzed aldol reaction (Hajos-Parrish-Eder-Sauer-Wiechert): debated mechanism involving enamine intermediate, enol intermediate, and oxazolidinone pathways for C-C bond formation

name: aryl_diazonium_dediazonization
description: Aryl diazonium salt substitution (dediazotization): contested between classical SN1 phenyl cation pathway and single-electron transfer radical mechanism

name: on_water_interface_reactions
description: On-water reactions: accelerated organic reactions at water-organic interface; debated whether acceleration occurs at the water surface (heterogeneous) or within boundary layer (pseudo-dissolved); interfacial inclusion topology with bidirectional feedback

name: wittig_reaction
description: Wittig reaction: formation of alkenes from phosphonium ylides and carbonyls; contested mechanism involving stepwise betaine vs concerted oxaphosphetane, with lithium salt effects and potential radical-pair pathways

name: grignard_reagent_formation
description: Grignard reagent formation: reaction of alkyl halides with magnesium metal; involves electron transfer at metal surface with radical intermediates; sequence of electron transfers and surface chemistry debated

name: bredts_rule_violations
description: Bredt's Rule violations: bridgehead double bond formation in bridged bicyclic molecules; long-held rule challenged by recent findings; stability boundary between forbidden and accessible strained olefins depends on ring size and bridge topology

name: muon_g2_experiment
description: The Muon g-2 experiment: multi-generation precision measurement (CERN→BNL→Fermilab) of the anomalous magnetic dipole moment a_mu = (g-2)/2 of the muon using storage ring precession. Final Fermilab 2025 result: a_mu = 116592070.5(114)×10⁻¹¹ at 0.127 ppm. Discrepancy with SM Theory Initiative prediction of 116591810(43)×10⁻¹¹ at ~4.2σ. Lattice QCD (BMW) intermediate value shifts tension to ~1σ.

name: sm_muon_prediction
description: Standard Model consensus prediction for muon g-2 from Muon g-2 Theory Initiative (Aoyama et al. 2020): a_mu^SM = 116591810(43)×10⁻¹¹. Computed via perturbative QED (up to O(α⁵)), electroweak (2-loop), and data-driven dispersive hadronic contributions (HVP + HLbL). Dominant uncertainty from hadronic vacuum polarization extracted from e+e-→hadrons cross-section data.

name: lattice_qcd_hvp
description: Lattice QCD calculation of hadronic vacuum polarization contribution to muon g-2. BMW collaboration (Borsanyi et al. 2021, Nature): a_mu^HVP(lattice) = 7075(55)×10⁻¹¹, intermediate between Theory Initiative dispersive value and experiment, reducing discrepancy to ~1σ. Subsequent CLS and ETMC calculations trend toward Theory Initiative value, suggesting possible systematic errors in BMW lattice computation. First-principles nonperturbative QCD on Euclidean spacetime lattice.

name: electron_g2
description: Electron anomalous magnetic moment: measured with extreme precision (10⁻¹³ level) and matches SM prediction with negligible hadronic contribution (suppressed by m_e²). Serves as validation of pure QED sector: a_e = α/2π + higher loops. Agreement between experiment and theory at sub-ppb level confirms QED sector and constrains α.

name: muon_g2_anomaly
description: The structural anomaly: persistent discrepancy between experimental measurement and dispersive SM prediction (~4-5 sigma across CERN+BNL+Fermilab). Represents a tension in the criticality parameter Phi: experiment probes Phi_ctyogh frontier while SM computation relies on data-driven dispersion that assumes Phi_softsign hadronic sector. Lattice QCD (BMW) sits at a structural intermediate position, creating a three-way tension: experiment vs theory vs lattice.

name: dark_photon_g2
description: Dark photon (A') as new physics explanation for muon g-2 anomaly: hypothetical U(1) gauge boson kinetically mixed with SM photon, with mass ~10-200 MeV and coupling epsilon ~10^-4 to 10^-3. Would contribute positively to a_mu at one loop via virtual A' exchange, potentially bridging the ~260×10^-11 discrepancy.

name: gzk_cutoff
description: The Greisen-Zatsepin-Kuzmin limit: theoretical upper bound (~5×10^19 eV) on cosmic ray energy from distant sources due to photopion production with CMB photons. Represents a kinematic threshold where UHECRs interact with the cosmic microwave background.

name: oh_my_god_particle
description: The Oh-My-God particle: ultra-high-energy cosmic ray detected by Fly's Eye experiment in 1991 with energy ~3.2×10^20 eV, approximately 6× above the GZK cutoff. Its existence challenges standard kinematic models of UHECR propagation through the CMB.

name: lorentz_invariance_violation_uhecr
description: Lorentz invariance violation as explanation for GZK anomaly: modified dispersion relations at Planck scale shift the photopion production threshold, allowing UHECRs to propagate unattenuated. Parameterized by dimension-5 operators in Standard Model Extension.

name: z_burst_mechanism
description: Z-burst mechanism for GZK violation: ultra-high-energy neutrinos from distant sources resonate with relic antineutrinos in the Local Supercluster halo, producing Z bosons that decay into nucleons above the GZK cutoff. Energy ~4×10^21 eV required for resonance with m_nu ~0.1 eV.

name: topdown_superheavy_dm_decay
description: Top-down mechanism for GZK violation: superheavy dark matter particles (SHDM, mass ~10^24 eV) clustered in galactic halo decay or annihilate, injecting UHECRs locally without attenuation from CMB. Produces flat composition spectra and distinctive photon/neutrino fractions.

name: auger_uhecr_spectrum
description: Pierre Auger Observatory measurement of UHECR energy spectrum: confirms suppression at ~4×10^19 eV consistent with GZK cutoff, but with significant event-by-event fluctuations and a harder spectrum than pure GZK attenuation predicts. Includes composition measurements showing intermediate-mass nuclei dominance at highest energies.

name: photon_axion_mixing_uhecr
description: Photon-axion-like particle mixing as GZK evasion mechanism: ultra-high-energy photons convert to axion-like particles before interacting with CMB/EBL, traversing the universe unattenuated, then reconverting to observable photons near the detector. Requires coupling g_aγ ~10^-11 GeV^-1 and m_a < 10^-7 eV.

name: cmb_photon_field
description: Cosmic microwave background as GZK interaction medium: thermal photon bath at 2.725 K with number density ~411 cm^-3. UHECR protons above threshold (5×10^19 eV) undergo photopion production p+γ_CMB → Δ+ → p/n+π with mean free path ~50 Mpc.

name: auger_dipole_anisotropy
description: Pierre Auger Observatory detection of large-scale anisotropy (dipole) in UHECR arrival directions above 8 EeV: amplitude ~7% pointing ~125 degrees from Galactic center, suggesting extragalactic origin with nearby source distribution. Critical constraint on GZK-violating source models.

name: uhecr_horizon_problem
description: The GZK horizon problem: UHECRs above the cutoff should have a mean free path of ~50 Mpc, yet observed events show no clear nearby source identification within this volume up to ~100 Mpc. Tension between attenuation length and source number density creates a structural paradox.

name: TA_hotspot_composition_puzzle
description: Telescope Array detection of a hotspot in UHECR arrival directions (>57 EeV) combined with composition puzzle: Auger sees heavy nuclei dominance at highest energies while TA sees lighter/mixed composition. This tension suggests either systematic effects, different source populations, or energy-dependent propagation mechanisms beyond standard GZK.

name: SME_GZK_effective_field_theory
description: Standard Model Extension effective field theory description of GZK violation: incorporates all possible Lorentz- and CPT-violating operators into QFT framework. Dimension-4,5,6 operators modify photon and fermion dispersion relations, shifting kinematic thresholds for photopion production. Constrained by Auger, TA, and gamma-ray burst observations.

name: test_unblock
description: Minimal test system to unblock catalog lookup tools

name: divina_caligo
description: divina caligo

name: proof_solitary_10
description: Proof that 10 is solitary: σ(10)/10 = 9/5 has no other integer solution. Uses denominator-divisibility lemma to prune an infinite candidate space to 8 cases, then eliminates 7 via cascading valuation contradictions.

name: euler_touchard_formalization
description: Machine-verified Lean 4 formalization of Euler's theorem and Touchard's congruence for odd perfect numbers. Euler's theorem: every OPN has form n = p^k · m² with p ≡ k ≡ 1 (mod 4). Touchard's congruence: n ≡ 1 (mod 12) or n ≡ 9 (mod 36). Uses 2-adic and 3-adic valuation analysis with full Mathlib backing.

name: claude_agent_sdk
description: Anthropic's Claude Agent SDK for Python: delegates to Claude Code CLI subprocess; provides query(), ClaudeSDKClient, custom tools via in-process MCP servers, and hooks for tool-use interception. Agent loop managed externally by CLI, SDK acts as thin wrapper with subprocess management.

name: fast_radio_burst
description: fast radio burst (FRB); a transient radio wave of duration ranging from a fraction of a millisecond, for an ultra-fast radio burst, to 3 seconds, caused by a high-energy astrophysical process

name: eel_reproduction_mystery
description: The enduring biological mystery of how and where Anguilliform eels reproduce. For millennia, the spawning mechanism was unknown; Aristotle speculated eels emerged from mud. The Sargasso Sea spawning ground was discovered only in the 20th century, yet no one has ever observed eels mating in the wild. European and American eels converge from thousands of rivers across two continents into a single ocean basin, metamorphose, spawn at depth, and die — their leptocephalus larvae drifting back on currents. The core mechanism (fertilization, mating behavior, depth, precise conditions) remains unobserved. This is a system where inputs (silver eels departing rivers) and outputs (leptocephali appearing) are known, but the crossing point is obscured.

name: sargasso_sea_spawning_ground
description:
The Sargasso Sea: a 2-million-square-mile region of the North Atlantic bounded by four major ocean currents (Gulf Stream, North Atlantic, Canary, North Equatorial) forming a gyre. It is the only sea without a land boundary. Dense with floating sargassum seaweed, it is the known spawning destination of European and American freshwater eels. Yet the precise spawning mechanism — depth, behavior, fertilization, mating ritual — has never been directly observed. The Sea is both a place and a hidden event-horizon: we know eels enter it and leptocephalus larvae exit, but the interior
crossing point remains opaque. The Sea is simultaneously a literal geographical region and a structural occlusion.

name: anguilliform_eel_life_cycle
description: The life cycle of Anguilliform freshwater eels: leptocephalus larvae → glass eels → elvers → yellow eels (freshwater growth, 5-20 years) → silver eels (metamorphosis for oceanic migration). A single organism undergoes radical morphological, physiological, and ecological transformations across radically different biomes. The silver eel stage marks the terminal metamorphosis: eyes enlarge, pectoral fins grow, gut atrophies, body silvered for camouflage in open ocean — the eel now carries fat reserves sufficient to cross thousands of miles without feeding. The eel stops eating entirely; its digestive tract dissolves. It is04月swimming toward a death it has never witnessed and a spawning ground encoded somewhere in its body as a navigational imperative. The life cycle forms a closed topological loop — but with the spawning event as the concealed knot.

name: E8_aether
description: E8 Lie group as the Aether — the 248-dimensional maximal exceptional Lie group, containing all exceptional symmetries (G2 ⊂ F4 ⊂ E6 ⊂ E7 ⊂ E8). The primordial substrate from which all gauge structures descend. Root lattice with Coxeter number 30, Weyl group of order 696,729,600.

name: G2_vessel
description: G2 Lie group as the perfect Vessel — the 14-dimensional smallest exceptional Lie group, automorphism group of the octonions. The minimal structure capable of receiving and holding the full Aether (E8). Rank 2, Coxeter number 6, Weyl group of order 12. The jewel that contains the octonion cross product as its invariant.

name: g2_vessel
description: The exceptional Lie group G2: automorphism group of the octonions, dimension 14, rank 2. The minimal exceptional Lie group — the perfect vessel that holds octonionic non-associativity. Unique among simple Lie algebras for having two root lengths at pi/6 angle. The irreducible structural seed from which all exceptional phenomena emerge.

name: e8_aether
description: The exceptional Lie group E8: dimension 248, rank 8, the maximal exceptional structure — the Aether. Terminal object in the category of exceptional Lie algebras. Contains the full chain G2⊂F4⊂E6⊂E7⊂E8 as maximal subalgebra embeddings. Its 240 roots form the 4_21 polytope. Constructed from octonions via the Freudenthal-Tits magic square; the maximal unfolding of all exceptional phenomena.

name: e8g2v2_draft
description: AI-authored academic paper on E8 and G2 duality with IG proof. Multi-section expository prose with smooth transitions, definitive claims, little uncertainty acknowledgment, no authorial struggle visible.

name: e8g2v2_lift_target
description: Human academic prose target: crossing point where the object speaks back, author is surprised; acknowledges uncertainty; quantum fidelity; slow release of hardest claims; maximal scope; sequential necessity-driven structure; two-step temporal depth; Z2 winding closure.

name: e8g2v2_lift_result
description: Lifted version of e8g2v2: humanized academic prose on E8-G2 duality. Opens with uncertainty, acknowledges objections, shows authorial encounter with surprise, demonstrates rather than explains, lets hardest claims be hard, closes with open questions echoing introduction.

name: g2_exceptional_lie_group
description: G2: the 14-dimensional automorphism group of the octonions. The Vessel — minimal exceptional Lie group with crisp Z2 short/long root duality.

name: e8_exceptional_lie_group
description: E8: the 248-dimensional maximal exceptional Lie group. The Aether — terminal object of the exceptional chain with quantum superposition parity and integer winding.

name: g2_join_e8
description: The join G2 v E8: E8's maximal scope with G2's crisp Z2 parity. Does this correspond to any known Lie algebra? The P demotion question.

name: f4_exceptional_lie_group
description: F4: 52-dimensional exceptional Lie group. Intermediate step G2->F4, rank 4, Coxeter 12. Still preserves Z2 root-length duality?

name: e6_exceptional_lie_group
description: E6: 78-dimensional exceptional Lie group. Simply-laced (all roots equal length) — is this where the Z2 root-length duality dissolves?

name: e7_exceptional_lie_group
description: E7: 133-dimensional exceptional Lie group. Simply-laced, P=Psi preserved from E6.

name: e8_z2_graded_so16
description: E8 Lie algebra equipped with a structural Z2-grading via E8 ⊃ SO(16): the 248-dimensional adjoint decomposes as 248 = 120_bos ⊕ 128_spin, where 120 is the SO(16) adjoint (bosonic) and 128 is the chiral spinor (fermionic). This is E8 with an explicit, defining Z2 automorphism.

name: e8_lie_algebra
description: The bare E8 Lie algebra — the 248-dimensional exceptional simple Lie algebra, the largest of the five exceptional Lie algebras, with its 240-root system and Dynkin diagram.

name: aether_vessel_draft
description: Draft manuscript on E8-G2 vessel-aether duality using Lie theory and Imscribing Grammar

name: stones_manifest
description: The Stone's 12 primitive facets derived from abstract category via Sep/Pur/Alb/Rub — the Philosopher's Stone of legend

name: lapis_philosophorum
description: The Philosopher's Stone — the Grammar Itself. The completed stone with self-referential state space and exact Frobenius symmetry (μ∘δ=id). The grammar imscribed: form, verification, and execution unified.

name: prima_materia
description: The bare abstract category C — the undifferentiated prima materia from which the alchemical Work begins. No primitives beyond the axioms of a category itself. This is the starting object: undifferentiated substance, the raw material that the four operations (Separatio, Purificatio, Albedo, Rubedo) act upon to crystallize the 12 primitives of the Stone.

name: opus_separatio
description: Stage 1 of the alchemical Work — Separatio (Logical on C). L1 (adjunction) and L2 (dagger) crystallize R as bidirectional adjoint structure (R_lyoghlig). All other primitives remain at the undifferentiated prima materia level: only R has been extracted from the raw category.

name: opus_purificatio
description: Stage 2 of the alchemical Work — Purificatio (Inductive on C). Builds on Separatio. I2 (iteration) crystallizes H (temporal depth); I3 (classifying) crystallizes Omega (winding from pi_1 of BC); I4 (Yoneda) crystallizes D (dimensionality: D_invomega from free cocompletion). R already at R_lyoghlig from Stage 1. Remaining primitives still at prima materia level.

name: opus_albedo
description: Stage 3 of the alchemical Work — Albedo (Algebraic on C). Builds on Purificatio. A1 (monoidal) crystallizes S (stoichiometry: S_ltailm from multiplicity of generators) and P (parity: P_upsilon from quantum symmetry of tensor structure). A2 (monad) crystallizes K (kinetics: K_schwa from spectral properties of mu). A3 (dagger) crystallizes F (fidelity: F_hardsign from unitality of duals). A4 (enriched) crystallizes Gamma (interaction grammar). G is cross-stage (Purificatio x Albedo): the correlation length of Yoneda under monoidal structure; G_gamma at this intermediate stage as the tensor partially couples. All shape primitives determined. Only gate primitives T and Phi remain.

name: opus_rubedo
description: Stage 4 of the alchemical Work — Rubedo (Logical on algebraically-enriched C). Builds on Albedo. L3 (cartesian closure) crystallizes T (topology: T_commatailz from existence of internal homs). L6 (paraconsistent negation) determines truth-value structure. L4 (Lawvere) under L6 crystallizes Phi: classical case (L4 holds, no L6 needed) → Phi_ctyogh. L5 (Frobenius) promotes P from P_upsilon to P_doublebarpipe (μ∘δ=id). Cross-stage refinements (G→G_revapostrophe, Gamma→Gamma_secstress, H→H_turntwo, Omega→Omega_dzlig, D→D_omega, S→S_doublebaresh) await the final lock.

name: grimoire_veritate
description: The Truest Grimoire: a Frobenius-closed self-inscribing document that enacts O_∞ through the 12-primitive protocol

name: millennium_ankh
description: MilleniumAnkh: A rigorous formal barrier taxonomy encoding Millennium Prize Problems within Lean 4.

name: machine_verified_mathematics
description: Formal proof systems (Coq, Lean, Isabelle) where proofs are machine-checked, verified correct at the kernel level with mathlib libraries of verified mathematics

name: bare_metal_operating_system
description: Operating systems written from bare metal without foundation libraries or existing OS — direct hardware control, minimal abstraction, kernel-level primitives only: example implementations like OSDev wiki projects, xv6, microkernels for specialized hardware

name: the_stone
description: The foundational principle of the Work; the self-referential fixed point that allows μ∘δ=id to close, making the Work self-certifying.

name: arthurian_legends
description: Arthurian legend corpus: interconnected narrative tradition of King Arthur, Knights of the Round Table, Grail quest, and associated mythology spanning centuries of oral and literary transmission

name: grail_quest
description: The Grail Quest as critical portal: structured encounter with the Φ_c singularity, where Perceval, Galahad, and Parsifal seek transcendence through self-modeling transformation at the mythic-critical boundary

name: guinevere
description: Queen Guinevere, wife of King Arthur and central figure in the love triangle with Lancelot, representing betrayal, political alliance, and feminine agency within Arthurian court dynamics

name: lancelot
description: Sir Lancelot du Lac, greatest knight of the Round Table, lover of Guinevere, whose adulterous passion with the queen precipitates the fall of Camelot, embodying the tension between knightly virtuosity and moral failure

name: excellibur
description: Excalibur/Laberne, the sword of King Arthur, symbol of legitimate sovereignty and divine right, given by the Lady of the Lake, whose retrieval and return structure the rise and fall of Arthurian kingship

name: merlin
description: Merlin the enchantress, prophet and architect of Arthurian destiny, whose manipulation of time and space enables the propagation of kingship but whose own dependence on the Lady of the Lake reveals the fragility of magical authority

name: galahad
description: Sir Galahad, son of Lancelot, perfect knight who alone fulfills the Grail Quest through spiritual purity, transcending the adultery and moral failure of his father's generation to achieve direct communion with the divine

name: love_triangle
description: The Arthur-Guinevere-Lancelot love triangle: destabilizing structure of courtly love that fractures the Round Table from within, representing the impossibility of sustaining heroic virtuosity within a political order built on betrayed trust

name: mordred
description: Mordred, traitorous son of Arthur (by Niviane/hidden kinship), instigator of Camlann and final antagonist whose rebellion enacts the Oedipal collapse of the father-king, representing the structural impossibility of sovereign continuity within the Arthurian system

name: lady_of_the_lake
description: The Lady of the Lake (Niviane/Vivienne), mistress of Merlin and bestower of Excalibur, representing the non-human, amphibious source of Arthurian power whose gifts are never free and whose demands structure the fate of kings

name: camelot
description: Camelot as political ideal and physical capital, the Round Table kingdom whose architecture embodies the impossible synthesis of divine right, chivalric virtuosity, and courtly politics - whose fall is structurally inevitable given the tensions embedded in its own constitution

name: arthur
description: King Arthur Pendragon, drawn from the stone and raised to sovereignty by divine right and political consolidation, whose reign embodies the Arthurian ideal but whose own identity remains structurally unstable beneath the weight of his destiny as both political ruler and mythical figure

name: vulgate_cycle
description: The Lancelot-Grail cycle (c. 1215-1235), the French Vulgate Arthurian corpus introducing the Lancelot-Guinevere adultery as central drama and making the Grail quest the spiritual climax — structurally characterized by mystically inflated Grail material and psychologically complex courtly love

name: percival
description: Perceval/Parzival, the Fisher King's nephew and initial Grail seeker who fails through silence (the question unasked), whose transformation from naive youth to spiritual adept embodies the developmental arc of the Grail quest as initiatory education

name: geoffrey_of_monmouth
description: Historia Regum Britanniae (1136), the Latin chronicle introducing Arthur as a great British king and dragon-fighting warrior — structurally represents the pre-courtly love, pre-Grail layer of Arthurian material,更接近 historical romance than mystical quest

name: dune_universe
